title: Manuscript Validation Report: FA4 B200 White-Box Runtime Predictor Draft
summary: Internal validation of the draft-section-set manuscript against the claim-evidence boundary, section writing plan, and oral-quality gates.

claim_support.notes: All numerical claims trace to validation_metrics.json or combined_predictions.csv. Combined MAPE 4.50%, baseline MAPE 22.22%, delta 17.72 pp, 100% within 30%, 100% bottleneck accuracy, and per-precision/per-bottleneck breakdowns are all inside the claim-evidence boundary. No unsupported real-silicon claims appear.

citation_legitimacy.notes: Citations are listed as placeholders with arXiv IDs from the paper-view citation needs (FA4 2603.05451v1, Blackwell microbenchmarks 2512.02189v1, white-box methodology 2605.04178v1). Full BibTeX entries were not generated in this stage and should be added during review or finalization.

figure_readiness.notes: Figures 1, 2, and 3 are referenced and described but not rendered. The draft includes explicit descriptions of what each figure would show. Rendering is deferred to isomer-deepsci-paper-plot or isomer-deepsci-figure-polish.

section_coverage.notes: All sections from the paper outline are present: Abstract, Introduction, Related Work, Method, Experiments, Results, Limitations, Conclusion. Tables 1-4 are included as Markdown tables.

language_hygiene.notes: Draft avoids operator, agent, route-control, worktree, and local runtime details. Claims are calibrated with explicit scope and emulator caveat. No strict-dominance language beyond evidence support.

bundle_readiness.notes: Draft is coherent and internally validated. Remaining gaps (BibTeX, rendered figures) do not block review. The paper bundle checkpoint references this report and the draft.

remaining_blockers.blocker: Ground truth is emulator-based, not silicon
remaining_blockers.resolution_route: future transfer-validation experiment if real B200 measurements become available
